vBRIEFInfo.author: overdeck/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-2063: Collapse UAT stack by default in dashboard

plan.title: Collapse UAT stack by default in dashboard
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-8

narratives.Problem: The 'UAT environment' panel inside an expanded feature row (FeatureItem.tsx:1282-1298) always renders its full per-service list. The header draws a ChevronDown that is purely decorative — no toggle is wired. Worse, UatStackStatus.tsx:122 forces showDetails=true whenever the stack is unhealthy, so the noisiest case (e.g. 0/7 healthy) is always fully expanded, cluttering a surface that is rarely the active focus.
narratives.Proposal: Make the wrapper (FeatureItem) own a session-local uatExpanded state defaulting to collapsed, and keep UatStackStatus a pure presenter (unchanged). Convert the uatStackTreeHeader <div> to a <button> that toggles uatExpanded, exposes aria-expanded, and renders ChevronRight when collapsed / ChevronDown when expanded — mirroring the existing row-caret pattern at FeatureItem.tsx:1080-1086. Render <UatStackStatus> only when uatExpanded; keep the 'UAT environment' label and the N/M-healthy summary span in the header so health stays glanceable while collapsed. Add a button-reset to .uatStackTreeHeader so it looks the same but behaves as a toggle. Update the existing FeatureItem UAT test (which currently asserts the expanded body by default) to assert collapsed-by-default plus click-to-expand, in the same change.
narratives.NonGoals:
- No localStorage persistence of the UAT collapse state (session-local useState only; a fresh render starts collapsed).
- No change to UatStackStatus.tsx (showDetails, summary, density behavior all stay).
- No change to the one-line 'feature-uat-stack' badge (FeatureItem.tsx:1203) or the outer feature-row caret.
- No change to the workspace-health query gating (FeatureItem.tsx:1028-1030); it must keep running while the panel body is collapsed so the header summary count stays populated.
- No change to which issues show the UAT panel (still readyForMerge + hasWorkspace + row expanded).

autoDecisions.summary: Wrapper owns collapse; UatStackStatus stays a pure presenter (do not edit UatStackStatus.tsx showDetails).
autoDecisions.rationale: Matches the issue's recommended fix; the existing feature-uat-stack badge + header summary keep health glanceable while collapsed, so the inner showDetails logic only needs to govern the already-mounted expanded view.

autoDecisions.summary: Session-local useState only — no localStorage persistence of the UAT collapse state.
autoDecisions.rationale: The issue marks persistence explicitly optional. The literal requirement is 'collapsed by default'; for a clutter-reduction feature, starting every fresh render collapsed is the cleaner default. Reusing readExpanded/writeExpanded would collide with the row-expand key (mc-feature-expanded:), so persistence would need a new key/helpers — added surface for an optional behavior, deferred to NonGoals.

autoDecisions.summary: Single bead covering FeatureItem.tsx + command-deck.module.css + FeatureItem.test.tsx.
autoDecisions.rationale: The existing UAT test (FeatureItem.test.tsx:626) asserts the expanded body by default and breaks under collapse-by-default. Impl and test must land together to keep the vitest suite green and the bead independently mergeable; splitting them would ship a red suite.

items.title: Default the UAT environment panel to collapsed with a working header toggle

narrative.Action: In src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/CommandDeck/ProjectTree/FeatureItem.tsx: add `const [uatExpanded, setUatExpanded] = useState(false);`. At the UAT panel render site (around lines 1282-1298), convert the `uatStackTreeHeader` <div> into a <button> that calls `setUatExpanded(v => !v)`, sets `aria-expanded={uatExpanded}` and an aria-label/title like 'Toggle UAT environment details', and renders `{uatExpanded ? <ChevronDown size={12} /> : <ChevronRight size={12} />}` (both already imported at line 5). Keep the 'UAT environment' label span and the `uatStackTreeSummary` span (`uatStackSummary.label.replace(/^UAT stack\s*/i, '')`) inside the header so the N/M-healthy count stays visible when collapsed. Render `<UatStackStatus ... density="compact" />` only when `uatExpanded` is true. Do NOT modify UatStackStatus.tsx, the feature-uat-stack badge (line 1203), or the workspace-health query gating (lines 1028-1030 — it must keep running while collapsed). In src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/CommandDeck/styles/command-deck.module.css, give `.uatStackTreeHeader` a button reset (appearance:none; background:none; border:none; width:100%; cursor:pointer; font:inherit; text-align:left; plus existing padding) while preserving its muted, uppercase, letter-spaced flex styling per pan-style-guide, and add a subtle hover color. Finally, update the existing test at src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/CommandDeck/ProjectTree/FeatureItem.test.tsx:626 ('shows expanded UAT environment state for ready-for-merge issues') to reflect collapsed-by-default: assert the 'UAT environment' header and feature-uat-stack badge render on mount while service rows ('postgres','api') and 'UAT stack 1/2 healthy' are absent, then fire a click on the UAT header and assert the service rows now render.
narrative.Why: Reduces visual clutter on a surface that is rarely the active focus, and stops the unhealthy case from being forced fully expanded, while keeping the health count glanceable when collapsed.

items.title: Given a ready-for-merge feature row with an unhealthy UAT stack and the row expanded, when it first renders, then the UAT panel renders collapsed — the 'UAT environment' label and the 'N/M healthy' header summary are present while the per-service rows (e.g. 'postgres', 'api') and the UatStackStatus body are not rendered.
When the user clicks the UAT panel header, then the UatStackStatus per-service detail list renders; clicking the header again removes it.

The UAT panel header renders as a button that exposes aria-expanded and a chevron reflecting state — ChevronRight when collapsed, ChevronDown when expanded — and preserves the existing muted, uppercase header styling.

FeatureItem.test.tsx renders the UAT panel collapsed on mount (no 'postgres'/'api' service rows) and renders those service rows after a click on the UAT header; npm run typecheck and npm run lint produce no new errors and the frontend vitest suite is green.
